title: Loops
summary: Repeat actions efficiently using for and while loops

sections.title: Loops Are Assembly Lines
sections.content: A loop is like an assembly line in a factory: the same action repeats on every item that passes by. Instead of writing the same code ten times for ten items, you write a loop that runs once per item. JavaScript has two main loops: for (when you know how many times to repeat) and while (when you repeat until a condition changes). Both save you from repetitive, error-prone manual coding.

sections.title: Counting with a for loop
sections.content: Prints numbers 1 through 5 using a for loop with a counter variable

sections.title: Loop Flow: Start, Check, Run, Update
sections.content: Every loop follows the same four steps in a cycle. First, the initial setup runs (like let i = 0). Next, the condition is checked (i < 5). If true, the code block runs. After the block finishes, the update step runs (i++), and then the condition is checked again. This continues until the condition becomes false. Understanding this cycle helps you avoid infinite loops — when the condition never becomes false and your program runs forever.

sections.title: Creating an infinite loop
sections.content: The #1 mistake beginners make is forgetting to update the loop variable, causing an infinite loop. Wrong: let i = 0; while (i < 5) { console.log(i); } — i is never incremented, so i < 5 is always true. Correct: let i = 0; while (i < 5) { console.log(i); i++; }. Always verify your loop variable changes toward the exit condition.

sections.title: Your turn
sections.prompt: A bakery bakes 12 cookies per batch. Use a loop to print the total number of cookies after each batch from batch 1 to batch 5. The output should show 'After batch 1: 12 cookies', 'After batch 2: 24 cookies', and so on.
hints: Should you use a for loop or a while loop here — which one fits 'I know exactly how many times'?
How do you calculate the running total inside the loop?
What is the relationship between the batch number and the formula for total cookies?
